title: Asking Directions
description: Find your way, ask for landmarks, and take a jeepney or tricycle in the Philippines.
situation: Getting around: asking where places are, how far, and which ride to take.

phrases.tip: Swap istasyon for any place: palengke, simbahan (church), banko.

phrases.english: How do I get to the mall?

phrases.tip: Opposite is malapit (near): malapit po ba?
A direction you'll hear constantly from locals.

phrases.native: Anong jeep po ang papunta sa Cubao?
phrases.pronunciation: a-NONG jip po ang pa-POON-ta sa koo-BA-o
phrases.tip: The jeepney is the classic, colourful public transport.
Shout this to make a jeepney or bus stop. Essential!
Bababa famously has three ba syllables in a row.
Tricycles handle short trips into smaller streets.

phrases.native: Ligtas po ba dito sa gabi?
phrases.english: Is it safe here at night?
phrases.pronunciation: lig-TAS po ba DEE-to sa ga-BEE
